messages.content:
The object of a statistical process control (SPC) system is to

provide a signal when assignable causes of variation are present.

assess the customer expectations.

provide a signal when natural causes of variation are present.

eliminate natural causes of variation.
provide a signal when assignable causes of variation are present.

Which of the following statement is true?

None of the above responses

A defect item contains many defectives

Defect and Defective are synonyms

A defective item contains many defects
A defective item contains many defects.

The (X, MR) Control Chart should be used when the sample size is:

equal to 0

more than 2

equal to 1

equal to 30

The SPC based on two process properties. These two properties are:

Stability and effectiveness.

Robustness and Efficiency.

hole diameter

Stability and Capability.

Which of the following statements is correct?

Every LP problem has at least one optimal solution

If a feasible region is unbounded then LP problem has no solution

Branch and Bound method can be used to solve LP problem

If an LP problem has two optimal solutions, then it has infinitely many solutions.
If an LP problem has two optimal solutions, then it has infinitely many solutions.

Linear programming (LP) can be applied effectively only if:

the objective can be stated theoretically.

resources can be measured as quantities.

resources can be measured as word.

formulate a mathematical model.
Resources can be measured as quantities.

A feasible solution to a LP problem…

must optimize the value of the objective function.

need not satisfy all of the constraints, only some of them.

must satisfy all the constraints of the problem simultaneously.

must be a corner point of the feasible region.
Must satisfy all the constraints of the problem simultaneously.

In goal programming, which of the following statements is correct?

Goal programming solutions are always optimal because they use linear programming constraints.

In goal programming, at least one of deviational variables in a goal constraint must equal zero.

The column-dropping rule is used to solve a goal programming formulation whatever preemptive method or weight method is used.

In goal programming, the preemptive method and the weight method produce the same solution, because these two methods use the same decision preferences.
In goal programming, at least one of deviational variables in a goal constraint must equal zero.

Consider the following problem: given a set of cities and distances between every pair of cities, the problem is to find the shortest possible route that visits every city exactly once and returns to the starting point. What is the name of this problem?

Shortest-route problem.

Travelling salesman problem.

Minimal spanning tree problem.

Minimum cost network problem.
